title: Predicting protein-protein interactions from primary structure
summary: Seminal early paper addressing prediction of protein-protein interactions directly from primary structure and associated data. Used SVM with physicochemical features extracted from protein sequences to classify interacting vs. non-interacting pairs. One of the first systematic ML approaches to PPI prediction from sequences.
datasets: Yeast two-hybrid data, DIP

title: MINT: a Molecular INTeraction database
summary: Original publication of MINT (Molecular INTeraction database), designed to store functional interactions between proteins extracted from peer-reviewed literature. Beyond binary complexes, MINT also stores enzymatic modifications and other functional interactions. Initial release contains 4568 interactions.
datasets: MINT v1.0 (4568 interactions)

title: A Bayesian Networks Approach for Predicting Protein-Protein Interactions from Genomic Data
summary: Seminal application of Bayesian networks to PPI prediction genome-wide in yeast by integrating diverse genomic data sources (gene expression, synthetic lethality, protein similarity, cellular localization). Demonstrated that Bayesian integration provides more accurate predictions at given sensitivity levels than individual high-throughput datasets. Established the framework for multi-evidence Bayesian PPI prediction.
datasets: S. cerevisiae genome-wide data (expression, genetics, essentiality)

title: STRING: a database of predicted functional associations between proteins
summary: Original STRING database publication. Provides integrated protein-protein interaction predictions with confidence scores from multiple genomic context methods (coexpression, gene fusion, genome context). Covers 261,033 orthologs from 89 genomes with expected accuracy of at least 80% at confidence threshold. Foundational resource for functional PPI analysis.
datasets: STRING v1 (261,033 proteins, 89 genomes)

title: The Database of Interacting Proteins: 2004 update
summary: Key update of DIP (Database of Interacting Proteins), one of the earliest protein-protein interaction databases. Contains experimentally determined binary interactions with quality assessment methods to identify a reliable CORE subset. DIP is used as training/test data in over 10 PPI prediction papers and as a benchmark for method evaluation.
datasets: DIP (>18500 binary PPIs as of 2003)

title: IntAct: an open source molecular interaction database
summary: First publication of IntAct, an open-source database for protein interactions at the EMBL-EBI. Provides storage, presentation and analysis tools for molecular interaction data. Contains ~2200 binary and complex interactions manually curated from literature using controlled vocabularies. Became one of the leading PPI data resources through collaboration with IMEx consortium.
datasets: IntAct v1 (~2200 interactions)

title: Kernel methods for predicting protein-protein interactions
summary: Proposed pairwise kernel methods for predicting protein-protein interactions using multiple data sources including protein sequences, Gene Ontology annotations, local network properties, and homologous interactions. Introduced the pairwise kernel that converts a single-protein kernel into a protein-pair kernel for SVM classification. Applied to yeast using BIND database.
datasets: BIND yeast PPI database, DIP, MIPS

title: Random forest similarity for protein-protein interaction prediction from multiple sources
summary: Presented Random Forest method for integrating heterogeneous biological data sources to predict PPIs. Constructed a forest of decision trees to compute similarity between protein pairs and used a modified kNN classifier for interaction prediction. Demonstrated the power of RF for handling missing values and weighting diverse data sources (gene expression, domain interactions, GO annotations).
datasets: Yeast PPI data from DIP, BIND; gene expression, TAP-MS data

title: Predicting protein-protein interactions using signature products
summary: Proposed a general sequence-based method for predicting PPIs using 'signature products' — a concatenation of amino acid composition descriptors combined with experimental interaction screen data. Applied to yeast and H. pylori. Showed that pairwise spectrum kernel with 3-mers provides good performance. Widely used as a benchmark baseline method.
datasets: Yeast PPI dataset, H. pylori interactions

title: Predicting protein-protein interactions based only on sequences information
venue: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
summary: Developed conjoint triad method for PPI prediction using only sequence information. Groups 20 amino acids into 7 classes based on dipoles and side chain volumes, then represents protein pairs using a 343-dimensional conjoint triad descriptor. Combined with SVM kernel function to achieve prediction accuracy of ~83.9% on human PPIs. Widely cited as a landmark sequence-only approach.
datasets: Human PPI dataset (~16,000 pairs), H. pylori dataset

title: IntAct—open source resource for molecular interaction data
summary: Major update of IntAct database with >126,000 binary interactions from >2100 publications. Features detailed experimental annotation including methods, conditions and interacting domains. Adopted PSI-MI standards and joined IMEx consortium. Became the backbone for MINT and other interaction databases.
datasets: IntAct 2007 (>126,000 interactions)

title: Using support vector machine combined with auto covariance to predict protein-protein interactions from protein sequences
summary: Introduced auto covariance (AC) descriptor combined with SVM for sequence-based PPI prediction. AC captures interactions between residues a certain distance apart in the sequence (up to 30 adjacent amino acids), overcoming the limitation of the conjoint triad method which only considers nearest neighbors. Achieved 88.09% prediction accuracy on independent yeast PPI dataset.
datasets: S. cerevisiae DIP data (5943 pairs training, 11474 test)

title: Accurate prediction of protein-protein interactions from sequence alignments using a Bayesian method
summary: Bayesian network method that predicts interaction partners directly from multiple alignments of amino acid sequences without tunable parameters or training examples. Applied to bacterial two-component signaling systems, achieving high accuracy in reconstructing interaction networks across sequenced bacteria. Also demonstrated on polyketide synthases.
datasets: Bacterial two-component signaling systems across sequenced genomes

title: iRefIndex: A consolidated protein interaction database with provenance
summary: iRefIndex: consolidated protein interaction database that integrates records from BIND, BioGRID, DIP, HPRD, IntAct, MINT, MPact, MPPI and OPHID. Provides unique provenance-tracked keys for interaction records using protein sequence and taxonomy identifiers. Enables cross-database comparison and redundancy detection across multiple PPI resources.
datasets: Integrated from 9 major PPI databases

title: Prediction of Protein-Protein Interaction Sites in Sequences and 3D Structures by Random Forests
summary: Combined sliding window approach with Random Forests to predict protein-protein interaction sites using both sequence and structural parameters. Achieved precision of 84% and recall of ~26% for sequence-based prediction; combining with structural information significantly improved recall. Demonstrated the complementarity of sequence and structure features for PPI site prediction.
datasets: Non-redundant PPI site dataset from PDB complexes

title: Human Protein Reference Database—2009 update
summary: Key update of HPRD (Human Protein Reference Database), the largest manually curated database of human protein interactions. Contains 38,167 protein-protein interactions, 16,972 post-translational modifications, subcellular localizations, and disease associations. Added PhosphoMotif Finder and NetPath pathways. Widely used as gold standard for human PPI prediction.
datasets: HPRD 2009 (38,167 PPIs, curated human proteins)

title: Protein-protein interaction databases: keeping up with growing interactomes
summary: Comparative review and analysis of six major PPI databases (BioGRID, MINT, BIND, DIP, IntAct, HPRD). Integrated and compared all PPI data, finding non-trivial overlap and complementarity between databases. HPRD found most comprehensive for human data. Recommends combining all databases for complete coverage.
datasets: BioGRID, MINT, BIND, DIP, IntAct, HPRD (compared)

title: The Negatome database: a reference set of non-interacting protein pairs
summary: Negatome: collection of protein and domain pairs unlikely to engage in direct physical interaction. Derived from manual literature curation and analysis of protein complexes with known 3D structure. Less biased toward functionally dissimilar proteins than random negative sets based on cellular localization. Essential resource for training and evaluating PPI prediction algorithms.
datasets: Negatome v1 (manually curated and structure-derived non-interacting pairs)

title: Prediction of Protein-Protein Interaction Sites by Random Forest Algorithm with mRMR and IFS
summary: Novel PPI site predictor using Random Forest with minimum redundancy maximum relevance (mRMR) feature selection and incremental feature selection (IFS). Incorporated physicochemical properties, sequence conservation, residue disorder, secondary structure, solvent accessibility, and 3D structural features. Showed 3D structural features (depth index, surface curvature) contribute most to prediction.
datasets: PDB-derived non-redundant PPI interface dataset

title: SKEMPI: a Structural Kinetic and Energetic database of Mutant Protein Interactions
summary: Original SKEMPI database: 3047 binding free energy changes upon mutation for 158 protein-protein complex structures from PDB. More than four times more data than previously collected. Used to investigate structural principles governing how mutations influence PPI binding and for training empirical binding affinity scoring functions.
datasets: SKEMPI v1 (3047 ΔΔG measurements, 85 protein-protein complexes)

title: Negatome 2.0: a database of non-interacting proteins derived by literature mining, manual annotation and protein structure analysis
summary: Major update of Negatome with >300% growth in content. Introduced advanced text mining (modified Excerbt tool with semantic sentence analysis) to guide manual annotation of non-interacting protein pairs. Negatome 2.0 provides a critical resource for negative training data in PPI prediction methods.
datasets: Negatome 2.0 (literature + structure-derived, grown 300%+ from v1)

title: Predicting protein–protein interactions from sequences using evolutionary profiles and profile-kernel SVMs
summary: This paper introduces a systematic difficulty classification framework for PPI prediction (C1/C2/C3 classes) and proposes a new method using evolutionary profiles with profile-kernel SVMs. It significantly improves over the state-of-the-art especially for C3 class proteins (sequence-dissimilar to training data), pushing AUC from 59% to 67% for human C3 interactions. The study definitively showed that most computational PPI methods fail on out-of-distribution proteins and established benchmark standards for cross-validation that account for sequence similarity bias. Gene expression filtering further increases accuracy for the most reliable interactions. The C1/C2/C3 difficulty classification has become a standard benchmark protocol adopted by subsequent PPI prediction papers to ensure fair evaluation and prevent overoptimistic reporting from sequence-similar train-test splits.
datasets: PINA human PPI (v3/2010), yeast PPI, UniProt sequences

title: STRING v10: protein–protein interaction networks, integrated over the tree of life
summary: STRING v10 covers more than 2000 organisms with protein-protein interaction data. Introduced novel algorithms for scaling to large numbers of organisms. Provides comprehensive scoring and integration of both direct physical and indirect functional interactions.
datasets: STRING v10 (>2000 organisms)

title: Algorithmic approaches to predict protein-protein interaction sites
summary: Systematic review of algorithmic approaches to PPIS (protein-protein interaction site) prediction, covering datasets, features, and methodology. Provides comparative analysis of state-of-the-art methods. Discusses utility for drug design, hotspot prediction, and computational docking. Comprehensive survey of classical ML approaches for PPI site identification.
datasets: N/A (review paper)

title: Ens-PPI: A Novel Ensemble Classifier for Predicting the Interactions of Proteins Using Autocovariance Transformation from PSSM
summary: Ensemble classifier combining autocovariance (AC) features extracted from Position-Specific Scoring Matrices (PSSM) for PPI prediction. Demonstrates that PSSM-derived AC features outperform raw sequence AC features, showing the value of incorporating evolutionary information through PSSMs.
datasets: S. cerevisiae, H. pylori, C. elegans, H. sapiens PPI datasets

title: Boosting Prediction of Protein–Protein Interactions with Deep Neural Networks
venue: Journal of Chemical Information and Modeling
summary: DeepPPI is a deep neural network method for protein-protein interaction prediction that learns discriminative features from common protein sequence descriptors using two architectures: DeepPPI-Sep (two separate networks per protein) and DeepPPI-Con (single concatenated network). The model automatically extracts high-level features through layer-wise abstraction without manual feature engineering. Tested on human and yeast PPI datasets, DeepPPI achieves accuracy of 92.50%, precision 94.38%, recall 90.56%, MCC 85.08%, and AUC 97.43%, outperforming existing methods based on SVM and other classifiers at the time of publication. The two-step architecture (DeepPPI-Sep and DeepPPI-Con) highlights that training two separate networks per protein before combining enables better feature disentanglement compared to a single concatenated network trained end-to-end.
datasets: Pan's human PPI dataset, S. cerevisiae dataset from DIP

title: SPRINT: ultrafast protein-protein interaction prediction of the entire human interactome
summary: SPRINT (Scoring PRotein INTeractions) is a sequence-based algorithm for predicting protein-protein interactions that exploits the hypothesis that similar protein pairs are more likely to interact. The key innovation is simultaneous computation of all interaction scores using spaced seeds stored in hash tables, enabling orders-of-magnitude speedup over previous methods. SPRINT is the only sequence-based program that can effectively predict the entire human interactome (requiring 15-100 min). It outperforms state-of-the-art methods on 7 reliable human PPI datasets in accuracy while using very little memory. SPRINT's key algorithmic innovation is the simultaneous computation of all pairwise scores using spaced-seed hash tables, fundamentally different from prior pair-by-pair evaluation approaches that made human interactome-scale prediction impractical.
datasets: 7 human PPI datasets including BioGRID, DIP, HPRD, IntAct, MINT

title: Accurate prediction of protein–protein interactions by integrating potential evolutionary information embedded in PSSM profile and discriminative vector machine classifier
summary: PSSM-based method for PPI prediction using an Information-theoretic Wavelet Lifting Descriptor (IWLD) to capture evolutionary information from position-specific scoring matrices combined with a Discriminative Vector Machine (DVM) classifier. Applied to Yeast and H. pylori datasets. One of several influential papers in the PSSM+SVM tradition for sequence-based PPI prediction.
datasets: S. cerevisiae yeast PPI dataset, H. pylori dataset

title: The BioGRID interaction database: 2017 update
summary: BioGRID 2017 update containing 1,072,173 genetic and protein interactions, 38,559 post-translational modifications, from 66 model organisms. Curated from 48,114 publications. Added chemical-protein interactions for drug targets drawn from DrugBank.
datasets: BioGRID v3.4 (1.07M interactions, 66 organisms)

title: The OncoPPi network of cancer-focused protein–protein interactions to inform biological insights and therapeutic strategies
summary: Reports generation of OncoPPi, a cancer-focused PPI network identifying >260 cancer-associated PPIs not present in other large-scale interactomes. PPI hubs reveal new regulatory mechanisms for cancer genes like MYC, STK11, RASSF1, and CDK4. Implemented using TR-FRET technology platform for systematic binary PPI discovery in a high-throughput format. Results inform cancer biology and therapeutic target identification.
datasets: Cancer gene PPI network, TR-FRET assay data

title: Predicting protein–protein interactions through sequence-based deep learning
summary: This paper presents DPPI, a deep learning framework for protein-protein interaction prediction using sequence information alone. The method employs a deep Siamese-like convolutional neural network combined with random projection and data augmentation, leveraging PSSM evolutionary profiles. DPPI outperforms state-of-the-art methods by 15-20% in terms of auPR and is 15 times computationally faster. The model predicts both heterodimeric and homodimeric interactions and was applied to cytokine-receptor binding affinity prediction. A key innovation is the random projection module that explores combinations of protein motifs from two sequence profiles. The model uses random projection to map learned protein motif representations to independent spaces, enabling efficient handling of 100,000+ training PPIs while remaining competitive for cytokine-receptor binding affinity estimation.
datasets: Human PPI dataset, S. cerevisiae dataset, cytokine-receptor binding dataset

title: Deep Neural Network Based Predictions of Protein Interactions Using Primary Sequences
summary: DNN-PPI presents a deep neural network framework for PPI prediction using only primary protein sequences, combining CNN and LSTM layers to learn representations without intensive feature engineering. When trained on Pan's human PPI dataset, the model achieves 98.78% accuracy and MCC of 97.57%. Evaluated on 6 external datasets from different species, the prediction accuracies ranged well above baseline methods. The study demonstrates remarkable generalization across species without retraining, suggesting the DNN can capture fundamental sequence patterns underlying protein interactions that are evolutionarily conserved. The model's cross-species generalization without retraining across six external datasets from different organisms suggests the DNN captures universally conserved sequence-level determinants of protein interactions rather than organism-specific patterns.
datasets: Pan's human PPI dataset, 6 external cross-species test datasets

title: Completing sparse and disconnected protein-protein network by deep learning
summary: This paper addresses PPI prediction from training networks that are sparse and disconnected, a major practical challenge. The method uses a deep neural network with an autoencoder-like architecture combined with a regularized Laplacian kernel to simulate evolutionary processes of PPI networks. The approach predicts interactions for proteins poorly covered by existing experimental data by leveraging structural and functional similarities as edge weights. Tested on human, yeast, and fly PPI networks, the method significantly improves prediction for low-coverage proteins compared to traditional similarity-based approaches. The combination of deep autoencoder-based network simulation with regularized Laplacian kernel weighting allows leveraging indirect structural and functional information about proteins not well-represented in current interactome maps.
datasets: Human PPI (BioGRID), S. cerevisiae, D. melanogaster interactomes

title: Multifaceted protein–protein interaction prediction based on Siamese residual RCNN
summary: PIPR (Protein-Protein Interaction Prediction based on Siamese Residual RCNN) is an end-to-end deep learning framework for PPI prediction from protein sequences alone. The model uses a residual recurrent convolutional neural network in a Siamese architecture, pre-trains amino acid embeddings to capture physicochemical properties, and leverages both local features and contextualized sequential information. Evaluated on binary PPI prediction, interaction type classification, and binding affinity estimation, PIPR outperforms state-of-the-art systems especially on multi-species datasets. A key strength is its ability to generalize to different PPI task types without significant reengineering. The Siamese architecture captures mutual influence between protein pairs without requiring predefined interaction features, and PIPR is the first method to unify binary classification, interaction type prediction, and binding affinity estimation in one framework.
datasets: Guo yeast dataset, multi-species dataset (H. pylori, C. elegans, H. sapiens), SHS27k, SHS148k

title: Multimodal deep representation learning for protein interaction identification and protein family classification
summary: First multi-modal deep representation learning framework for PPI prediction combining stacked auto-encoder architecture with CBOW-based metapath representations over knowledge graphs. Integrates sequence, structural, and network-based protein features via multi-modal deep neural network achieving superior performance over single-modal methods.
datasets: Human PPI network, STRING database, protein feature databases

title: An Efficient Feature Extraction Technique Based on Local Coding PSSM and Multifeatures Fusion for Predicting Protein-Protein Interactions
summary: LCPSSMMF method that combines local coding PSSM with multifeatures fusion (Local Average Group and Bigram Probability). Integrates global and local feature extraction from PSSM while accounting for interactions between residues in both continuous and discontinuous regions. Demonstrates strong performance on yeast and human PPI datasets.
datasets: S. cerevisiae yeast PPI, Homo sapiens PPI

title: STRING v11: protein–protein association networks with increased coverage, supporting functional discovery in genome-wide experimental datasets
summary: STRING version 11 update more than doubling the number of organisms covered. Covers >1100 organisms with comprehensive protein-protein association data combining physical and functional interactions from multiple sources. Added novel scalable algorithms for transferring interaction information between organisms.
datasets: STRING v11 (>1100 organisms)

title: SE(3)-Transformers: 3D Roto-Translation Equivariant Attention Networks
summary: SE(3)-Transformers combine the expressiveness of attention mechanisms with SE(3)-equivariance for 3D point cloud and molecular structure processing. Applied to protein structure tasks including interaction prediction. Key building block for subsequent equivariant architecture advances including Invariant Point Attention in AlphaFold2.
datasets: QM9, N-body particle simulations, protein structure benchmarks

title: Amalgamation of 3D structure and sequence information for protein-protein interaction prediction
summary: Proposes fusion of 3D protein structure information with sequence features using deep learning for PPI prediction. Extracts representations from protein 3D coordinates using hydropathy and other physicochemical features, combines with sequence features through a deep learning pipeline. Demonstrates effectiveness of multimodal structural and sequence fusion for PPI.
datasets: Human PPI dataset, Yeast PPI dataset, custom curated PPI benchmarks

title: D-SCRIPT translates genome to phenome with sequence-based, structure-aware, genome-scale predictions of protein–protein interactions
summary: D-SCRIPT is a deep learning model for predicting physical protein-protein interactions from amino acid sequences using a structurally motivated design. The model uses a pre-trained protein language model to generate rich representations, then infers inter-protein contact maps as an intermediate structural output. This approach improves cross-species generalizability since protein structure is more evolutionarily conserved than sequence. A D-SCRIPT model trained on 38,345 human PPIs achieves improved functional characterization of fly proteins, predicts genome-wide PPIs in cow, and its contact map output correlates with known 3D complex structures. The structurally-motivated contact map intermediate output provides interpretability absent in most black-box deep learning models, allowing biologists to inspect predicted contacting residue pairs and assess biological plausibility of predicted interactions.
datasets: Human PPI (BioGRID), D. melanogaster interactome, B. taurus proteome, STRING

title: Protein interaction interface region prediction by geometric deep learning
summary: PInet (Protein Interface network) combines geometric deep learning on molecular surfaces with physicochemical complementarity for interface region prediction. Leverages best properties of both data- and physics-driven methods capturing geometric and chemical surface complementarity to improve precision and recall in PPI site prediction.
datasets: Benchmark2, Docking Benchmark 5.0, CASP-CAPRI targets

title: Fast end-to-end learning on protein surfaces
summary: dMaSIF (differentiable MaSIF) introduces point-cloud-based operators for protein surface learning that replace the expensive mesh generation of MaSIF. Achieves 10-100x speedups while maintaining competitive accuracy for PPI site prediction, protein-protein interaction matching, and protein-small molecule binding. Uses implicit surface representations and orientation-aware point convolutions.
datasets: MaSIF-site, MaSIF-search, PDB benchmarks

title: Learning from Protein Structure with Geometric Vector Perceptrons
summary: Introduces Geometric Vector Perceptrons (GVPs) that extend standard neural network layers to operate on collections of Euclidean vectors. GVP-GNNs perform both geometric and relational reasoning on protein structure for model quality assessment and protein design. Combines scalar and vector features preserving SO(3) equivariance without tensor products.
datasets: ATOM3D, CATH protein structure dataset

title: Equivariant Graph Neural Networks for 3D Macromolecular Structure
summary: Extension of GVP to full-atom representations of macromolecular structures including protein-protein and protein-ligand complexes. Demonstrates equivariant message passing for interface contact prediction and binding affinity estimation with explicit 3D coordinate processing maintaining SE(3)-equivariance.
datasets: PDB protein complexes, ATOM3D datasets

title: Invariant Point Attention and SE(3)-equivariant structure modules in AlphaFold2
summary: AlphaFold2's structure module introduces Invariant Point Attention (IPA), a geometry-aware SE(3)-invariant attention mechanism that processes 3D protein backbone frames. IPA enables the model to reason about spatial relationships between residues while maintaining rotational and translational invariance, a crucial architectural contribution applied broadly in protein interaction modeling.
datasets: PDB, CASP14 benchmark

title: The BioGRID database: A comprehensive biomedical resource of curated protein, genetic, and chemical interactions
summary: Comprehensive update of BioGRID (Biological General Repository for Interaction Datasets), the most widely used curated database for protein and genetic interactions. Contains ~1.93 million curated interactions from >63,000 publications across major model organisms and humans. Also captures post-translational modifications and protein-chemical interactions. Widely used as gold standard for ML PPI prediction.
datasets: BioGRID v4 (1.93M interactions, 66 species)

title: RCSB Protein Data Bank: powerful new tools for exploring 3D structures of biological macromolecules
summary: Major update of the RCSB Protein Data Bank (PDB), the global archive for 3D structure data of biological macromolecules. Completely redesigned web portal with Boolean search, integration with >40 external biodata resources. Essential resource for structure-based PPI prediction, providing 3D coordinates of protein complexes used to derive training data for structural ML methods.
datasets: PDB (>180,000 structures including protein complexes)

title: Network medicine framework for identifying drug-repurposing opportunities for COVID-19
venue: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences (PNAS)
summary: Deployed AI, network diffusion, and network proximity algorithms to rank 6,340 drugs for expected efficacy against SARS-CoV-2. Experimentally screened 918 drugs to evaluate drug-repurposing methodologies and used a consensus algorithm to improve accuracy. Network drugs that reduced viral infection did not bind SARS-CoV-2 target proteins directly, revealing network-based mechanisms beyond docking strategies.
datasets: Human PPI network, SARS-CoV-2 interactome, DrugBank

title: EMOGI: multi-omics integration via graph convolutional networks for cancer driver gene prediction
summary: EMOGI integrates multi-omics data (mutations, copy number variations, DNA methylation, gene expression) with PPI networks using graph convolutional networks (GCNs) in a semi-supervised learning framework to predict cancer driver genes. Outperforms existing methods and identifies novel driver genes including potential cancer therapeutic targets. Provides interpretable predictions via node masking and feature attribution.
datasets: TCGA pan-cancer multi-omics, STRING PPI network

title: Uncovering cancer vulnerabilities by machine learning prediction of synthetic lethality
summary: Describes PARIS (PAn-canceR Inferred Synthetic lethalities), a machine learning approach to identify cancer vulnerabilities. PARIS predicts synthetic lethal (SL) interactions by combining CRISPR viability screens with genomics and transcriptomics data. Unraveled a dependency between ALDH2 and BRIP1, identifying BRIP1 as a potential therapeutic target in ~30% of all tumors.
datasets: CRISPR viability screens, TCGA genomics and transcriptomics

title: Network-based protein-protein interaction prediction method maps perturbations of cancer interactome
summary: Established NECARE, a novel cancer-specific PPI prediction method based on relational graph convolutional network (R-GCN) with knowledge-based features. Achieves MCC of best performance and F1=91% compared to other methods. Mapped the cancer interactome atlas revealing that perturbations of PPIs were enriched on 1362 cancer hub genes.
datasets: Cancer genomics data, BioGRID PPI, COSMIC cancer mutations

title: Topsy-Turvy: integrating a global view into sequence-based PPI prediction
summary: Topsy-Turvy synthesizes sequence-based bottom-up and network topology top-down approaches in a multi-scale deep learning model for PPI prediction. Using a transfer-learning framework, it incorporates global network patterns from GLIDE (a spectral link predictor) during training while making predictions from sequence data only. This multi-objective training balances structural and topological considerations. In cross-species settings, Topsy-Turvy achieves state-of-the-art performance on five model organisms for genome-scale PPI prediction. The TT-Hybrid variant, combining Topsy-Turvy with GLIDE link predictions, further outperforms constituent components for species with existing PPI data, addressing both well-characterized and poorly-characterized proteins. Topsy-Turvy is feasible for whole-genome screens where structure-based methods like AlphaFold-Multimer would be computationally infeasible.
datasets: Human, E. coli, D. melanogaster, C. elegans, S. cerevisiae STRING PPI networks

title: Deep learning frameworks for protein–protein interaction prediction
summary: This comprehensive review paper covers deep learning architectures applied to PPI prediction including CNNs, RNNs, graph neural networks, transformers, and autoencoders. The paper benchmarks major methods including DPPI, PIPR, D-SCRIPT, and graph-based approaches on standardized datasets. It discusses feature embedding strategies from amino acid composition to protein language models, benchmark datasets from DIP/BioGRID, and extended applications in drug discovery and functional annotation. A key contribution is the comparative analysis showing that sequence-based methods still lag structure-aware and network-aware approaches. The review provides guidance on appropriate benchmarking protocols to avoid data leakage through sequence similarity, and identifies the transition from hand-crafted features to learned representations as the defining advance in the field.
datasets: Survey of multiple PPI datasets from DIP, BioGRID, IntAct, STRING

title: Geometric Transformers for Protein Interface Contact Prediction
summary: Presents the Geometric Transformer within the DeepInteract framework for rotation and translation-invariant partner-specific protein interface contact prediction. Given 3D structures of two proteins, predicts inter-protein residue-residue contacts. Demonstrates superior performance on CASP-CAPRI and Docking Benchmark 5.5 targets.
datasets: Docking Benchmark 5.5, CASP-CAPRI, PDB interface structures

title: ScanNet: an interpretable geometric deep learning model for structure-based protein binding site prediction
summary: ScanNet is an interpretable geometric deep learning architecture for protein binding site prediction from 3D structure. Uses spatial convolutions over point cloud representations of protein environments, learning to identify atoms and residue patterns associated with binding. Achieves state-of-the-art performance on PPI site, antibody paratope, and nanobody prediction.
datasets: PDB binding site benchmarks, antibody-antigen, nanobody datasets

title: OntoProtein: Protein Pretraining With Gene Ontology Embedding
summary: OntoProtein pre-trains protein language models with Gene Ontology (GO) knowledge graph embeddings via novel contrastive learning with knowledge-aware negative sampling. Jointly optimizes protein sequence embeddings and biological knowledge representations during pre-training. Outperforms state-of-the-art PLMs on TAPE benchmark and shows improved PPI prediction.
datasets: Swiss-Prot, Gene Ontology, TAPE benchmark

title: Structure-Aware Multimodal Deep Learning for Drug-Protein Interaction Prediction
venue: Journal of Chemical Information and Modeling
summary: STAMP-DPI is a structure-aware multimodal deep learning model for drug-protein interaction (DPI) prediction. Combines predicted contact map-based GNN for protein structure representation with pre-trained molecular embeddings. Includes high-quality GalaxyDB benchmark and achieves state-of-the-art performance on Davis and GalaxyDB datasets with interpretable binding site prediction.
datasets: GalaxyDB, Davis, KIBA datasets

title: Multi-omics PPI prediction with knowledge graph integration for disease mechanism discovery
summary: Multi-omics approach integrating genomic, transcriptomic, and proteomic data with biological knowledge graphs for comprehensive PPI prediction in disease contexts. Combines heterogeneous multi-omics features via attention-based fusion with knowledge graph embeddings, enabling discovery of disease-relevant protein interaction modules.
datasets: Multi-omics disease databases, STRING, BioGRID, OMIM

title: Robust deep learning based protein sequence design using ProteinMPNN
summary: ProteinMPNN is a deep learning protein sequence design method using message passing neural networks that processes protein backbone geometry. Achieves 52.4% sequence recovery on native backbones. Supports coupled multi-chain design enabling protein-protein interface design. Validated by X-ray crystallography and cryo-EM for monomers, oligomers, nanoparticles, and binders.
datasets: PDB training set, CATH, experimental validation proteins

title: Protein Structure and Sequence Generation with Equivariant Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models
summary: Introduces a generative model for joint protein structure and sequence design using equivariant denoising diffusion probabilistic models. Conditions generation on compact protein topology specifications to produce full-atom backbone configurations, sequences, and side-chain predictions. Enables protein complex interface design via conditioned generation.
datasets: PDB protein structures, CATH, experimental validation

title: Benchmark Evaluation of Protein–Protein Interaction Prediction Algorithms
summary: Systematic re-implementation and evaluation of 36 published PPI prediction algorithms on realistic datasets with appropriate class distributions. Found that performance of most sequence-only methods is overstated due to protein over-representation bias and scale-free network properties. Many methods failed to outperform naive control models based on protein frequency. Calls for standardized evaluation practices.
datasets: Human PPI from BioGRID, DIP; Park and Marcotte benchmark sets

title: Protein–protein interaction prediction with deep learning: A comprehensive review
summary: Comprehensive review of deep learning methods for PPI prediction, covering prediction of protein functions, protein-protein interactions and their sites, protein-ligand binding, and protein design. Reviews both sequence-based and structure-based deep learning approaches. Provides systematic taxonomy of DL architectures applied to PPI tasks.
datasets: N/A (review paper)

title: MODIG: integrating multi-omics and multi-dimensional gene network for cancer driver gene identification based on graph attention network model
summary: MODIG uses a graph attention network (GAT) to identify cancer driver genes combining multi-omics pan-cancer data with multi-dimensional gene networks. Establishes diverse types of gene relationship maps based on PPI, gene sequence similarity, KEGG pathway co-occurrence, gene co-expression patterns, and Gene Ontology. Applies GAT to model within-dimension interactions and joint learning to fuse dimension-specific representations for semi-supervised driver gene identification. Outperforms baselines in AUPR and AUROC.
datasets: TCGA pan-cancer multi-omics, STRING PPI, KEGG, GO databases

title: ProtInteract: A deep learning framework for predicting protein–protein interactions
summary: ProtInteract is a two-stage deep learning framework for PPI prediction combining a novel autoencoder architecture and a deep convolutional neural network. The autoencoder encodes each protein's primary structure to a lower-dimensional vector while preserving sequence attributes, and the CNN predicts interactions with confidence scores under three different scenarios. The model enables fast training by compressing protein representations and outputs confidence scores indicating probability of interaction. The framework is evaluated across multiple datasets achieving competitive performance while being computationally efficient. The confidence-score output (rather than binary classification) allows users to threshold predictions based on desired precision-recall trade-offs, making it adaptable for different screening applications.
datasets: Human PPI datasets, S. cerevisiae, multiple cross-species benchmarks

title: State-of-the-art computational methods to predict protein–protein interactions with high accuracy and coverage
summary: This comprehensive review covers sequence-based, structure-based, and co-abundance-based computational approaches for PPI prediction, with particular focus on how deep learning advances each data type. The paper organizes methods taxonomically by primary data source and evaluates their strengths and weaknesses. Key analyses include the impact of AlphaFold structures on structure-based prediction, limitations of co-expression features, and the challenge of integrating multi-modal data. The review identifies that network-aware methods combining sequence and interaction topology consistently achieve the highest accuracy and coverage. Special attention is given to the shift from PSSM features to protein language model embeddings and how this transition has reshaped performance benchmarks across all prediction paradigms.
datasets: Review of multiple PPI databases and benchmarks

title: PeSTo: parameter-free geometric deep learning for accurate prediction of protein binding interfaces
summary: PeSTo is a parameter-free geometric transformer that accurately identifies protein binding interfaces from experimentally solved and predicted (AlphaFold) structures. Processes protein atomic coordinates and element types without requiring alignment or sequence profiles, enabling efficient interface prediction across the growing foldome.
datasets: PDB protein complexes, AlphaFold predicted structures

title: ProteinMAE: masked autoencoder for protein surface self-supervised learning
summary: ProteinMAE introduces a masked autoencoder pre-training strategy for protein molecular surfaces, addressing label scarcity in surface-based protein learning. The model learns informative surface representations capturing chemical and geometric properties, then fine-tunes for PPI prediction and protein design tasks with limited labels.
datasets: PDB molecular surfaces, MaSIF benchmarks

title: GeoBind: segmentation of nucleic acid binding interface on protein surface with geometric deep learning
summary: GeoBind applies geometric deep learning to predict nucleic acid binding sites on protein surfaces via a segmentation approach. Takes whole-protein point clouds as input and aggregates neighbors in local reference frames. Outperforms state-of-the-art methods on benchmark datasets for protein-nucleic acid binding site prediction.
datasets: DNA/RNA binding benchmark datasets from PDB

title: Protein Representation Learning by Geometric Structure Pretraining
summary: GearNet proposes pre-training protein representations through 3D structural self-supervised learning. Uses edge-enhanced graph neural networks with geometric features and multiple pretraining objectives including residue type prediction and angle prediction. Achieves strong performance on protein function prediction and fold classification with less pretraining data.
datasets: AlphaFold DB, SCOPe, GO benchmark, Enzyme Commission

title: G-RANK: an equivariant graph neural network for the scoring of protein-protein docking models
summary: G-RANK applies GVP-GNN (Geometric Vector Perceptron Graph Neural Network) to score protein-protein docking models. Uses equivariant message passing to evaluate candidate docked structures and rank them by nativeness. Achieves strong performance identifying near-native protein-protein complex structures from docking decoy sets.
datasets: Docking Benchmark 5.5, ZDOCK decoy sets

title: A Systematic Study of Joint Representation Learning on Protein Sequences and Structures
summary: Comprehensive study exploring joint protein representation learning by integrating protein language models (ESM-2) with structure encoders (GVP, GearNet, CDConv). Introduces three representation fusion strategies and evaluates on diverse downstream tasks including PPI. Finds that fusing sequence and structure representations outperforms either modality alone.
datasets: ATOM3D, GO, Enzyme Commission, protein fold classification

title: Multimodal pretraining for unsupervised protein representation learning
summary: Presents a multimodal protein pre-training framework combining sequences, graphs, and 3D point clouds using large language models and generative models. Introduces novel combination of representations from multiple sources into a single global protein representation. Evaluates on protein function prediction and interaction-related downstream tasks.
datasets: PDB, Swiss-Prot, protein function benchmarks

title: Protein Representation Learning via Knowledge Enhanced Primary Structure Modeling
summary: KeAP (Knowledge-exploited Auto-encoder for Protein) performs token-level knowledge graph exploration for protein representation learning. Non-masked amino acids iteratively query associated knowledge tokens from a protein knowledge graph to extract and integrate helpful information for restoring masked amino acids. Outperforms prior methods on 9 downstream applications.
datasets: Swiss-Prot, Gene Ontology KG, TAPE, FLIP benchmarks

title: De novo design of protein structure and function with RFdiffusion
summary: RFdiffusion fine-tunes RoseTTAFold on protein structure denoising tasks to obtain a generative model for protein backbones. Achieves outstanding performance on de novo binder design, symmetric oligomer design, enzyme active site scaffolding, and motif scaffolding. Experimentally validates hundreds of designed symmetric assemblies, metal-binding proteins, and binders with cryo-EM structure confirmation.
datasets: PDB training structures, experimental validation datasets

title: SE(3) diffusion model with application to protein backbone generation (FrameDiff)
summary: FrameDiff develops SE(3) diffusion models over multiple frames for protein backbone generation, introducing theoretical foundations for SE(3)-invariant diffusion without relying on pre-trained structure prediction networks. Generates designable monomers up to 500 amino acids and provides framework for protein complex backbone design via conditional generation.
datasets: PDB protein backbone structures, CATH

title: Illuminating protein space with a programmable generative model (Chroma)
summary: Chroma is a programmable generative model for proteins and protein complexes that samples novel structures and sequences conditioned on desired properties. Introduces a diffusion process respecting polymer conformational statistics and a low-temperature sampling algorithm. Achieves protein design as Bayesian inference under external constraints including symmetries, shape, semantics, and natural language prompts.
datasets: PDB, AlphaFold DB, experimental validation proteins

title: Diffusion probabilistic modeling of protein backbones in 3D for the motif-scaffolding problem
summary: SMCDiff learns a distribution over protein backbone structures via E(3)-equivariant GNN and develops efficient conditional sampling via sequential Monte Carlo. First method to theoretically guarantee conditional samples from protein diffusion models in large-compute limit. Addresses protein complex interface scaffolding by conditioning on binding motifs.
datasets: PDB protein structures, CATH, AlphaFold DB

title: DockGame: Cooperative Games for Multimeric Rigid Protein Docking
summary: DockGame frames rigid protein docking as a cooperative game, using two approaches: (1) computing equilibria via simultaneous gradient updates with Nash equilibrium games, and (2) sampling from a Gibbs distribution via diffusion generative model over rotation/translation action spaces. Shows significantly faster runtimes than traditional docking on Docking Benchmark 5.5.
datasets: Docking Benchmark 5.5 (DB5.5), PDB protein complexes

title: Fast protein backbone generation with SE(3) flow matching (FrameFlow)
summary: FrameFlow adapts FrameDiff to the flow-matching paradigm for protein backbone generation, requiring 5x fewer sampling timesteps while maintaining quality. Demonstrates how flow matching can be effectively applied on SE(3) with modified training for vector field learning. Extended to motif-scaffolding for protein complex binding site design.
datasets: PDB, CATH backbone structures, CAMEO evaluation

title: The STRING database in 2023: protein–protein association networks and functional enrichment analyses for any sequenced genome of interest
summary: Major update of the STRING database, the premier resource for protein-protein association networks. Covers 67 million proteins from 14,094 organisms. Integrates physical and functional interactions from text mining, computational predictions, co-expression, and experimental data. Adds functional enrichment analysis capabilities for any sequenced genome.
datasets: STRING v12 (67M proteins, 14094 organisms)

title: UniProt: the Universal Protein Knowledgebase in 2023
summary: Latest major update of UniProt Knowledgebase, the definitive resource for protein sequences and functional annotation. Contains >227 million sequences and >85% now linked to AlphaFold structure predictions. Used as the primary sequence source for virtually all computational PPI prediction methods. Reviewed (Swiss-Prot) entries provide gold-standard sequence and functional annotation.
datasets: UniProtKB (>227M sequences, >85% with AlphaFold structures)

title: Machine learning on protein–protein interaction prediction: models, challenges and trends
summary: Comprehensive survey of machine learning-based PPI prediction methods from 2020-2023. Reviews data sources, protein feature representations, ML model types (sequence-based, structure-based, hybrid), current challenges (data quality, negative sampling, generalizability), and future directions including using AlphaFold-predicted structures. Essential reference for state of the field.
datasets: N/A (review paper)

title: Recent Advances in Deep Learning for Protein-Protein Interaction Analysis: A Comprehensive Review
summary: Comprehensive review of deep learning applications for PPI analysis as of 2023. Covers CNNs, RNNs, graph neural networks, attention mechanisms and language model approaches applied to PPI detection, binding site prediction, and interaction modelling. Discusses evaluation metrics, benchmark datasets, and remaining challenges.
datasets: N/A (review paper)

title: Structural biology: The transformational era
summary: Editorial review by Shoshana Wodak on the transformational impact of AI (AlphaFold, RoseTTAFold) on structural biology and protein-protein interaction prediction. Reviews implications for improved PPI prediction benchmarking and the new era of structure-based interactome analysis. From a leading authority on protein docking and interaction prediction.
datasets: N/A (review/editorial)

title: Identification of essential genes associated with SARS-CoV-2 infection as potential drug target candidates with machine learning algorithms
summary: Proposed a method to highlight essential genes playing crucial roles in SARS-CoV-2 pathogenesis using PPI networks. Defined eleven informative topological and biological features for biological and PPI networks constructed on COVID-19 gene sets. Applied three unsupervised learning algorithms to rank important genes, identifying 18 important genes related to COVID-19 as potential drug targets.
datasets: COVID-19 gene sets, STRING PPI network

title: Explainable Multilayer Graph Neural Network for cancer gene prediction
summary: Introduces EMGNN, an Explainable Multilayer Graph Neural Network approach to identify cancer genes by leveraging multiple gene-gene interaction networks and pan-cancer multi-omics data. Unlike methods using a single biological network, EMGNN uses a multilayered GNN to learn from multiple biological networks simultaneously. Achieves 7.15% improvement in AUPR over state-of-the-art methods. Provides model-level feature importance and molecular-level gene set enrichment analysis.
datasets: Pan-cancer multi-omics (TCGA), STRING PPI, multiple interaction networks

title: KR4SL: knowledge graph reasoning for explainable prediction of synthetic lethality
summary: Knowledge graph reasoning approach for explainable synthetic lethality prediction in cancer. Aggregates messages and enhances sequential semantics of paths using a recurrent neural network with an attentive aggregator to identify critical subgraph structures. Predictions can unveil the process and mechanisms underlying synthetic lethality, demonstrating deep learning's utility for SL-based cancer drug target discovery.
datasets: Synthetic lethality databases, PPI networks, knowledge graphs

title: Disease gene prioritization with quantum walks
summary: Proposes a new algorithm for disease gene prioritization based on continuous-time quantum walks using the adjacency matrix of a PPI network. Compares the quantum walk method to several well-known gene prioritization methods on three disease sets across seven different PPI networks using cross-validation and mean reciprocal ranks. Performs enrichment analysis of predicted genes for coronary artery disease.
datasets: PPI networks (7 networks), disease gene datasets

title: Deploying synthetic coevolution and machine learning to engineer protein-protein interactions
summary: Created libraries of synthetic proteins with amino acids varying bidirectionally (coevolution), recovering matched pairs of coevolved proteins. Mapped sequences in a coevolutionary network and determined structures of 10 synthetic protein complexes. Harnessed pretrained protein language models to expand, in silico, the amino acid diversity, predicting remodeled interfaces beyond experimental library reach.
datasets: Yeast display library, protein complex structures

title: Improving Cancer Survival Prediction via Graph Convolutional Neural Network Learning on Protein-Protein Interaction Networks
venue: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
summary: Proposes CRESCENT, a PPI prior knowledge graph-based convolutional neural network (GCN) to improve cancer survival prediction. Relies on gene expression networks rather than gene expression levels alone. Evaluated on a pan-cancer dataset of 5,991 patients from 16 cancer types in TCGA. Demonstrates that incorporating network structure between genomic features effectively improves cancer survival prediction.
datasets: TCGA pan-cancer dataset (5991 patients, 16 cancer types), STRING PPI network

title: CFAGO: cross-fusion of network and attributes based on attention mechanism for protein function prediction
summary: CFAGO integrates single-species PPI networks and protein biological attributes via a multi-head attention mechanism for protein function prediction. Pre-trained with an encoder-decoder architecture, then fine-tuned for protein function prediction. Outperforms state-of-the-art single-species network-based methods by at least 7.59% (m-AUPR), 6.90% (M-AUPR), and 11.68% (Fmax) on human and mouse datasets.
datasets: Human and mouse PPI networks, UniProtKB/Swiss-Prot protein annotations

title: Improving the generalizability of protein-ligand binding predictions with AI-Bind
summary: Shows state-of-the-art deep learning models fail to generalize to novel (never-before-seen) drug and protein structures in drug-target interaction prediction. Introduces AI-Bind, a machine learning pipeline to improve generalizability and interpretability of binding predictions for poorly annotated proteins and small molecules. Demonstrates network-based approaches to overcome data limitations.
datasets: BindingDB, DrugBank, UniProtKB

title: Large language models generate functional protein sequences across diverse families
summary: ProGen2 - large language model for protein generation that generates functional protein sequences across diverse protein families. Generated functional lysozyme proteins from five distinct protein sequence families, with activities confirmed by cell-based assays. Demonstrates that LLMs can generate novel functional proteins far outside the training distribution, establishing foundational approach for protein language model-based protein design.
datasets: UniRef90, BFD, Pfam, NCBI protein sequences

title: Identifying Significantly Perturbed Subnetworks in Cancer Using Multiple Protein–Protein Interaction Networks
summary: Proposes MultiFDRnet to identify driver genes and pathways using multiple PPI networks jointly. The false discovery rate (FDR) refers to the proportion of non-cancer genes within identified subnetworks. Method detects significantly perturbed subnetworks jointly supported by multiple PPI networks and identifies novel modular structures in context-specific PPI networks. Tested on simulated and real cancer data.
datasets: Multiple PPI networks, TCGA cancer data

title: A multi-network approach to Alzheimer's Disease gene prioritization complements cis-regulatory prioritization with molecular quantitative trait loci
summary: Develops MNFP (Multi-Network Functional Prediction), a natural extension of NetWAS for gene prioritization that combines information from multiple tissue-specific networks simultaneously. Applied to Alzheimer's Disease GWAS data to prioritize candidate genes. Observes significant concordance between MNFP method and mQTL-based approach. Shows network-based functional predictions are a strong complement to mQTL-based approaches for AD genetics.
datasets: AD GWAS data, tissue-specific PPI networks, mQTL datasets

title: Deep representation learning of protein-protein interaction networks for enhanced pattern discovery
summary: DNE (Discriminative Network Embedding) is a self-supervised framework for learning protein representations in PPI networks. The model incorporates protein sequence features from protein language models (ESM-2) with network structural information using a discriminative contrastive learning approach. DNE substantially outperforms existing network embedding methods for PPI inference and functional module identification. Combining sequence features with network topology results in over 20% improvement in ROC-AUC for S. cerevisiae PPI prediction versus sequence-only approaches, demonstrating synergy between language model representations and network-level patterns. Integration of ESM-2 protein language model features with network structural information delivers >20% improvement in ROC-AUC compared to using sequence features alone, demonstrating that structural embeddings and network topology provide complementary information.
datasets: S. cerevisiae PPI, human PPI, multiple species from STRING

title: Cracking the black box of deep sequence-based protein–protein interaction prediction
summary: This critical benchmark paper systematically investigates how much deep learning PPI prediction models depend on data leakage and sequence homology artifacts. The authors find that many high-accuracy estimates are inflated due to train-test sequence similarity, and demonstrate that models primarily learn to recognize sequence similarity to known interactors rather than genuine interaction features. The paper introduces rigorous cross-validation protocols that prevent sequence-based leakage and shows that truly sequence-independent PPI prediction remains an unsolved challenge. Reproducible models are released to enable fair benchmarking. The authors release reproducible implementations with standardized benchmarking code, establishing a rigorous framework that other researchers can use for fair method comparison and setting a new standard for the field.
datasets: Human, yeast, and multiple organism PPI networks from BioGRID, IntAct, STRING

title: SENSE-PPI reconstructs protein-protein interactions of various complexities within, across, and between species with sequence-based evolutionary scale modeling
summary: SENSE-PPI is a sequence-based deep learning model using ESM-2 protein language model embeddings combined with a siamese transformer architecture to predict PPIs within, across, and between species. The model efficiently reconstructs ab initio PPIs and distinguishes interaction partners among tens of thousands of proteins, including functionally similar proteins. SENSE-PPI demonstrates the important role of language model parameter count on prediction quality. Benchmarked across multiple organisms, it shows superior performance for identifying specific interactions within protein families compared to homology-based approaches. SENSE-PPI achieves strong inter-species transfer by leveraging ESM-2's pre-training on evolutionary data from millions of protein sequences, circumventing the need for species-specific training data that limits other methods.
datasets: Human, yeast, E. coli PPI networks; cross-species benchmarks using STRING

title: MEG-PPIS: a fast protein-protein interaction site prediction method based on multi-scale graph information and equivariant graph neural network
summary: MEG-PPIS uses multi-scale graph information combined with E(n) equivariant graph neural networks for protein-protein interaction site prediction. Captures information at different graph scales using pooling, with weight-sharing EGNN for simultaneous processing. Achieves optimal performance across all metrics with fastest runtime compared to baselines.
datasets: Benchmark2, PSAIA, TEST_60 PPI site benchmarks

title: AtomSurf: Surface Representation for Learning on Protein Structures
summary: AtomSurf combines protein surface representations with graph-based architectures via learned feature exchange between surface and graph representations. Proposes direct integration of surface and atom-level information for protein function prediction and PPI tasks, improving over pure surface or pure graph approaches across ATOM3D benchmark.
datasets: ATOM3D benchmark suite, PDB protein structures

title: Exploiting Hierarchical Interactions for Protein Surface Learning
summary: HCGNet (Hierarchical Chemical and Geometric Feature Interaction Network) bridges chemical and geometric features with hierarchical interactions for protein surface analysis. Captures atom-level chemical features and hierarchical surface-atom residue interactions. Outperforms MaSIF by 2.3% on site prediction and 3.2% on interaction matching tasks.
datasets: MaSIF site and search benchmarks, PDB protein surfaces

title: Protein language model-embedded geometric graphs power inter-protein contact prediction
summary: PLMGraph-Inter combines protein language model embeddings with geometric graphs from interacting protein structures for inter-protein contact prediction. Uses rotationally and translationally invariant geometric graphs built from protein complex structures and integrates ESM language model features via GVP-based encoders and residual networks.
datasets: PDB protein-protein complex structures, inter-protein contact benchmarks

title: GOProteinGNN: Leveraging Protein Knowledge Graphs for Protein Representation Learning
summary: GOProteinGNN enhances protein language models by integrating Gene Ontology protein knowledge graph information at the amino acid level. Creates comprehensive protein representations combining evolutionary sequence context with biological knowledge graph relationships and dependencies between proteins and functional annotations.
datasets: Swiss-Prot, Gene Ontology graph, TAPE/FLIP benchmarks

title: CCPL: Cross-modal Contrastive Protein Learning
summary: CCPL introduces cross-modal contrastive protein learning for unsupervised structure representation pretraining. Uses a protein language model for sequence context and unsupervised contrastive alignment to enhance structure learning, incorporating self-supervised structural constraints. Improves downstream PPI and function prediction tasks.
datasets: PDB, AlphaFold DB, protein function benchmarks

title: Nanobody-antigen interaction prediction with ensemble deep learning and prompt-based protein language models
summary: Develops an ensemble deep learning framework combining prompt-based protein language models for nanobody-antigen interaction prediction. Integrates multiple sequence and structural modalities via ensemble strategy, demonstrating strong performance in predicting nanobody binding specificity with applications in antibody engineering.
datasets: Nanobody-antigen complex structures from PDB, SAbDab

title: De novo design of high-affinity protein binders with AlphaProteo
summary: AlphaProteo introduces a family of machine learning models for de novo protein binder design achieving 3-300x better binding affinities and higher experimental success rates than existing methods on seven diverse protein targets. Generates 'ready-to-use' binders for research applications using only one round of medium-throughput screening without further optimization.
datasets: PDB complex structures, experimental binding assay results for 7 targets

title: Generalized Biomolecular Modeling and Design with RoseTTAFold All-Atom
summary: RFAA extends RoseTTAFold to model full biological assemblies containing proteins, nucleic acids, small molecules, metals, and covalent modifications. Fine-tuned to develop RFdiffusionAA which generates protein binding pockets directly around small molecules. Designs and validates proteins binding digoxigenin, heme, and bilin molecules with accurate cryo-EM structures.
datasets: PDB full biological assemblies, experimental validation structures

title: SE(3)-Stochastic Flow Matching for Protein Backbone Generation (FoldFlow)
summary: FoldFlow introduces SE(3) stochastic flow matching for protein backbone generation, offering advantages over diffusion-based approaches: more stable/faster training and ability to map any invariant source to target distribution over SE(3). Generates high-quality designable, diverse, and novel protein backbones up to 300 amino acids enabling protein complex design.
datasets: PDB protein backbones, CATH, designability evaluation

title: Out of Many, One: Designing and Scaffolding Proteins at the Scale of the Structural Universe with Genie 2
summary: Genie 2 extends the original Genie diffusion model to design complex proteins engaging multiple interaction partners and performing multiple functions. Trained on the entire AlphaFold structural universe, achieves state-of-the-art performance on unconditional and conditional protein backbone generation including motif scaffolding and protein complex design.
datasets: AlphaFold DB structural universe, PDB

title: Protein structure generation via folding diffusion
summary: FoldingDiff presents a diffusion-based generative model for protein backbone structures that mirrors the natural folding process. Describes backbone structure as consecutive torsion angles and generates structures by denoising from random unfolded states. Produces diverse novel protein backbones for interface design scaffolds and complex assembly design.
datasets: CATH4.2 protein structures, designability evaluation benchmarks

title: ContactNet: Geometric-Based Deep Learning Model for Predicting Protein-Protein Interactions
summary: ContactNet develops an attention-based GNN for classifying protein-protein docking poses without requiring multiple sequence alignments. Works directly from 3D protein structures using geometric reasoning. Addresses the need for PPI scoring methods applicable to novel interactions where MSA-based co-evolutionary information is unavailable.
datasets: Docking Benchmark 5.5, ZDOCK decoy sets, PPI interaction databases

title: Unified Sampling and Ranking for Protein Docking with DFMDock
summary: DFMDock unifies structure generation and decoy ranking in protein-protein docking via denoising force matching. Predicts both denoising forces and scalar energy trained via force matching and energy contrastive objectives. Outperforms DiffDock-PP with 44% sampling success vs 8% on Docking Benchmark 5.5 and produces funnel-shaped energy landscapes.
datasets: Docking Benchmark 5.5, DIPS pre-training dataset

title: Pitfalls of machine learning models for protein–protein interaction sites prediction
summary: Important benchmarking study revealing pitfalls in ML models for PPI prediction. Developed B4PPI framework accounting for network topology biases, highly connected hub proteins, and training/test leakage. Shows complementarity of functional genomics vs. sequence-based models. Demonstrates models using functional genomics better suited for cross-species PPI prediction.
datasets: Human PPI (BioGRID), S. cerevisiae PPI; B4PPI benchmark framework

title: AI-guided pipeline for protein–protein interaction drug discovery identifies a SARS-CoV-2 inhibitor
summary: Comprehensive pipeline combining experimental and computational tools to identify and validate PPI targets for early-stage drug discovery. Developed a multi-adaptive SVM (maSVM) classifier to prioritize interactions using quantitative PPI assay data and AlphaFold-Multimer predictions. Applied to SARS-CoV-2 proteins to identify high-confidence interactions, then used VirtualFlow for ultra-large virtual drug screening of the NSP10-NSP16 methyltransferase complex, identifying a compound that disrupts the complex and SARS-CoV-2 replication.
datasets: LuTHy binary PPI assay data, AlphaFold-Multimer predictions, VirtualFlow compound library

title: Target repositioning using multi-layer networks and machine learning: The case of prostate cancer
summary: Addresses limitations of PPI-only drug target repositioning by exploiting a multi-layer network incorporating gene regulation, metabolite interactions, metabolic pathways, and disease signatures. Extracts features from multiple approaches including proximity to disease genes, propagation-based methods, topological metrics, and module detection. Identifies 5 novel promising therapeutic targets for prostate cancer: IGF2R, C5AR, RAB7, SETD2, and NPBWR1.
datasets: Prostate cancer multi-omics data, STRING PPI, KEGG pathways

title: MPASL: multi-perspective learning knowledge graph attention network for synthetic lethality prediction in human cancer
summary: MPASL uses multi-perspective learning knowledge graph attention network to enhance synthetic lethality prediction. Utilizes knowledge graph hierarchy propagation to explore multi-source neighbor nodes related to genes, and knowledge graph ripple propagation to expand gene representations through existing SL preference sets. Learns gene representations from both gene-entity perspective.
datasets: SynLethDB, cancer genomics, knowledge graph with gene ontology

title: Accurate structure prediction of biomolecular interactions with AlphaFold 3
summary: AlphaFold 3 predicts the joint structure of complexes including proteins, nucleic acids, small molecules, ions, and modified residues using a diffusion-based architecture. Significantly advances protein-protein and protein-ligand interaction prediction accuracy. Released as a web server and later partially open-sourced under restricted license (AlphaFold Server).
datasets: PDB structures, CCD ligand library, nucleic acids data

title: Boltz-1: Democratizing Biomolecular Interaction Modeling
summary: Boltz-1 is an open-source deep learning model achieving AlphaFold3-level accuracy in predicting 3D structures of biomolecular complexes. Incorporates innovations in model architecture, speed optimization, and data processing. Introduces Boltz-steering, an inference-time steering technique that fixes hallucinations and non-physical predictions. Released training/inference code, model weights, datasets, and benchmarks under MIT license.
datasets: PDB (training), custom biomolecular interaction benchmarks

title: Technical Report of HelixFold3 for Biomolecular Structure Prediction
summary: Replicates AlphaFold3 capabilities via the PaddleHelix team. HelixFold3 provides precise predictions for diverse protein complex structures, especially therapeutic protein interactions. Achieves remarkable success in antigen-antibody and peptide-protein structure prediction, greatly surpassing AlphaFold 3 on antigen-antibody interactions. Available publicly on the PaddleHelix platform with both general and antigen-antibody versions.
datasets: PDB structures, antibody-antigen structures

title: xTrimoPGLM: Unified 100B-Scale Pre-trained Transformer for Deciphering the Language of Protein
summary: xTrimoPGLM is the largest protein language model at 100 billion parameters trained on 1 trillion tokens, addressing both protein understanding and generation tasks simultaneously through a unified pre-training framework. Significantly outperforms other baselines on 18 protein understanding benchmarks across four categories. Facilitates atomic-resolution protein structure prediction surpassing other language model-based tools. Also performs programmable de novo protein sequence generation after supervised fine-tuning.
datasets: UniRef90, BFD, MGnify metagenomic sequences

title: Sequence modeling and design from molecular to genome scale with Evo
summary: Evo is a long-context genomic foundation model trained on millions of prokaryotic and phage genomes that generalizes across DNA, RNA, and proteins. Reports scaling laws for DNA foundation models. Enables zero-shot function prediction competitive with domain-specific language models and generates functional CRISPR-Cas and transposon systems. Represents first foundation model to span the continuum from molecular to genome-scale sequence modeling.
datasets: OpenGenome dataset (prokaryotic genomes), phage genomes

title: ProLLM: Protein Chain-of-Thoughts Enhanced LLM for Protein-Protein Interaction Prediction
summary: ProLLM employs an LLM for PPI prediction using Protein Chain of Thought (ProCoT), which replicates biological signaling pathways as natural language prompts. Considers a signaling pathway as a protein reasoning process from upstream to downstream proteins. Transforms structured protein data into natural language to allow LLMs to identify indirect connections through intermediate proteins. Achieves significant improvement over existing methods in prediction accuracy and generalizability.
datasets: STRING PPI database, signaling pathway databases

title: Prot2Token: A multi-task framework for protein language processing using autoregressive language modeling
summary: Prot2Token combines autoregressive language modeling with protein language models (PLMs) to tackle various protein prediction tasks using protein sequences. Adapts existing PLMs for multiple tasks including protein-level prediction, residue-level prediction, and PPI prediction through next-token prediction. Enables multi-task training in a single end-to-end session using prompt tokens. Uses Prot2Token to develop S-ESM, a structure-aware ESM model.
datasets: Multiple protein benchmark datasets

title: Foundation models in molecular biology
summary: Comprehensive review of foundation models in molecular biology, covering RNA sequence, DNA sequence, protein sequence, single-cell transcriptome, and spatial transcriptome models. Discusses research directions for development of foundation models in molecular biology. Contextualizes foundation models in PPI prediction, protein structure prediction, and functional genomics.
datasets: Review (no datasets)

title: Benchmarking AlphaFold3's protein-protein complex accuracy and machine learning prediction reliability for binding free energy changes upon mutation
summary: Independent validation of AlphaFold 3 predictions using SKEMPI 2.0 database (317 protein-protein complexes, 8338 mutations). AF3 complex structures give Pearson correlation of 0.86 for binding free energy changes upon mutation, slightly less than 0.88 from PDB structures. AF3 structures led to 8.6% increase in prediction RMSE compared to original PDB structures, and are not reliable for intrinsically flexible regions.
datasets: SKEMPI 2.0 database (317 complexes, 8338 mutations)

title: SpatialPPI: Three-dimensional space protein-protein interaction prediction with AlphaFold Multimer
summary: Proposes SpatialPPI, a highly accurate method for predicting protein-protein interactions using 3D structural information from AlphaFold Multimer. Uses the AlphaFold Multimer confidence metrics and 3D structural features as inputs to a deep neural network classifier to determine whether two proteins actually interact (binary PPI prediction). Addresses the limitation that AlphaFold Multimer cannot distinguish true interactors from non-interactors.
datasets: Human PPI benchmark datasets, AlphaFold Multimer predictions

title: Revolutionizing protein-protein interaction prediction with deep learning
summary: Reviews latest advances in computational methods for 3D protein complex modeling and prediction of protein interaction partners, emphasizing application of deep learning methods from coevolution analysis. Covers recent developments in AlphaFold-based methods, co-evolutionary approaches, and end-to-end deep learning for PPI prediction. Highlights biomedical applications of PPI prediction and outlines challenges in the field.
datasets: Review (no datasets)

title: GLIMS: A two-stage gradual-learning method for cancer genes prediction using multi-omics data and co-splicing network
summary: GLIMS is a two-stage gradual-learning strategy to predict cancer genes using multi-omics data. First stage uses a semi-supervised hierarchical GNN integrating multi-omics and PPI networks; second stage optimizes initial prediction by integrating co-splicing networks in post-transcriptional regulation. Outperforms state-of-the-art methods for cancer gene identification including EMOGI, MTGCN, and MODIG.
datasets: TCGA pan-cancer multi-omics, STRING PPI, co-splicing networks

title: Using protein language models for protein interaction hot spot prediction with limited data
summary: Demonstrates that protein language models (ESM-2) can effectively predict protein-protein interaction hot spots even with limited training data. Shows which sequence features are important for determining certain residue properties in PPI hot spot prediction. Addresses the challenge of small dataset sizes in protein interaction hot spot prediction with transfer learning from large-scale PLMs.
datasets: BioLip PPI hot spot datasets, limited experimental data

title: ProtLLM: An Interleaved Protein-Language LLM with Protein-as-Word Pre-Training
summary: ProtLLM is a versatile cross-modal LLM for both protein-centric and protein-language tasks. Features a dynamic protein mounting mechanism to handle complex inputs with natural language text interspersed with proteins. Proposes protein-as-word language modeling approach. Induces zero-shot and in-context learning capabilities on protein-language tasks. Demonstrates LLM-enabled PPI prediction and functional characterization.
datasets: InterPT (large-scale interleaved protein-text dataset), protein function databases

title: Deep learning in modeling protein complex structures: From contact prediction to end-to-end approaches
summary: Reviews recent advances in deep learning-based approaches for modeling protein-protein complex structures including inter-protein contact prediction and end-to-end approaches. Covers development from coevolutionary contact methods to AlphaFold-based modeling and other end-to-end deep learning frameworks. Discusses advantages, limitations, challenges, and future directions for protein complex structure prediction.
datasets: Review (no datasets)

title: Integration of molecular coarse-grained model into geometric representation learning framework for protein-protein complex property prediction
summary: MCGLPPI is a novel geometric representation learning framework combining GNNs with the MARTINI molecular coarse-grained (CG) model to predict PPI complex properties accurately and efficiently. Maps proteins onto a concise CG-scale complex graph with nodes as CG beads and edges encoding chemically plausible interactions. Achieves competitive performance vs. atom- and residue-scale counterparts with only 1/3 computational resource. CG-scale pre-training on protein domain-domain interaction structures enhances predictive capabilities.
datasets: PDBbind, SKEMPI, multiple PPI property benchmarks

title: Chai-1: Decoding the molecular interactions of life
