A key innovation is an interpretable attention mechanism that highlights physically important residue interactions—the network attends to proximal aromatics and hydrogen bonding partners that constrain CDR loop conformations. DeepAb outperforms leading alternatives on both structural diversity and clinical-stage therapeutic antibody benchmarks. Additionally, a mutant scoring metric derived from network confidence identifies mutations that experimentally improve binding affinity, bridging structure prediction with antibody engineering.
datasets: SAbDab benchmark (diverse + clinical therapeutics), mutagenesis validation

title: AbAgIntPre: A deep learning method for predicting antibody-antigen interactions based on sequence information
summary: AbAgIntPre is a sequence-only deep learning method for predicting antibody-antigen interactions (AAI) without requiring 3D structural information. A Siamese-like convolutional neural network architecture uses amino acid composition encoding for both antigens and antibodies to learn interaction features. The method addresses the structure availability bottleneck of prior methods that require experimental structures. AbAgIntPre demonstrates fast identification of potential antibody-antigen binding pairs from sequence alone, enabling high-throughput screening of large antibody repertoires against target antigens. It provides a complementary approach to structure-based methods, particularly valuable for novel antigens where no structural information is available.
datasets: PDB antibody-antigen complex pairs, custom positive/negative interaction dataset

title: deepHPI: a comprehensive deep learning platform for accurate prediction and visualization of host-pathogen protein-protein interactions
summary: deepHPI is a comprehensive deep learning platform for predicting host-pathogen protein-protein interactions (HP-PPIs), addressing the challenge that most known interactions are intra-species while pathogen-host interactions are critical for infection mechanisms. The platform employs multiple deep learning architectures to learn representations of both host and pathogen protein sequences, with specialized models for different pathogen types. The system includes visualization tools for interaction networks and supports analysis across multiple host-pathogen systems. Benchmarked against existing HP-PPI prediction methods, deepHPI achieves improved accuracy and broader coverage across diverse pathogen types, providing a platform for computational prioritization of potential drug targets in infectious disease.
datasets: HP-PPI databases (HPIDB, PHI-base), curated host-pathogen interaction datasets

title: HPiP: an R/Bioconductor package for predicting host-pathogen protein-protein interactions from protein sequences using ensemble machine learning
summary: HPiP is an R/Bioconductor software package for predicting host-pathogen PPIs from protein sequences using an ensemble of machine learning classifiers. The package implements a series of amino acid sequence property descriptors including physicochemical, evolutionary, and structural features, combined with ensemble classifiers (Random Forest, SVM, XGBoost) to predict unmapped interactions. Using SARS-CoV-1 and SARS-CoV-2 coronavirus-human PPI training sets, HPiP achieves good performance in identifying novel interactions. The package provides standardized preprocessing, model training, and prediction pipelines accessible within the Bioconductor ecosystem, enabling researchers without deep machine learning expertise to conduct HP-PPI prediction analyses.
datasets: SARS-CoV-1 and SARS-CoV-2 human PPI datasets, HPIDB

title: On the choice of negative examples for prediction of host-pathogen protein interactions
summary: This methodological analysis addresses a critical issue in host-pathogen PPI prediction: the choice of negative examples dramatically influences reported performance and can produce artificially inflated accuracy estimates. The paper demonstrates that the common practice of selecting negative examples with low sequence similarity to positives creates dataset bias, making the classification problem much easier than the real-world scenario. Using SARS-CoV-2 and other host-pathogen interaction datasets, the authors show that multiple recent HP-PPI prediction methods with high reported accuracy are largely solving the negative example selection artifact rather than the true interaction problem. The paper urges careful dataset construction and provides guidelines for unbiased evaluation.
datasets: SARS-CoV-2-human PPI data, multiple host-pathogen PPI datasets

title: LGCA-VHPPI: A local-global residue context aware viral-host protein-protein interaction predictor
summary: LGCA-VHPPI develops improved statistical representations for viral-host protein sequences by incorporating comprehensive local and global residue context, addressing limitations of doc2vec approaches that only capture local sequence context. Local residue context-aware encoding captures semantic relatedness and short-range dependencies, while global context captures long-range dependencies, positional invariance, and unique residue combination distributions. A deep forest classifier effectively models complex non-linearity in viral-host PPI sequence representations. LGCA-VHPPI outperforms the state-of-the-art predictor by 6%, 2%, and 2% in MCC across three benchmark viral-host PPI datasets, demonstrating the importance of multi-scale sequence context for cross-species interaction prediction.
datasets: Three benchmark viral-host PPI prediction datasets (HIV-human, Yersinia-human, Citrus-bacteria PPIs)

title: Fast, accurate antibody structure prediction from deep learning on massive set of natural antibodies (IgFold)
summary: IgFold is a fast deep learning method for antibody structure prediction using an end-to-end architecture consisting of a pre-trained antibody language model (trained on 558 million natural antibody sequences) followed by graph networks that directly predict backbone atom coordinates. IgFold predicts structures of similar or better quality than alternative methods including AlphaFold in under 25 seconds per structure. This speed enables previously infeasible analyses: the authors predicted structures for 1.4 million paired antibody sequences, providing structural insights to 500-fold more antibodies than have experimental structures. IgFold's speed-accuracy balance makes it the preferred tool for high-throughput antibody structural analysis and repertoire profiling.
datasets: Observed Antibody Space (OAS), 558M antibody sequences, SAbDab structural benchmark

title: ImmuneBuilder: Deep-Learning models for predicting the structures of immune proteins
summary: ImmuneBuilder is a set of deep learning models for accurate structure prediction of immune proteins: ABodyBuilder2 for antibodies, NanoBodyBuilder2 for nanobodies, and TCRBuilder2 for T-cell receptors. The models achieve state-of-the-art accuracy while being substantially faster than AlphaFold2—processing an antibody in ~4 seconds versus ~20 minutes. Each model provides per-residue error estimates, enabling confidence-based filtering and selection. ABodyBuilder2's specialized antibody-specific architecture and training data produces more accurate CDR loop predictions compared to general protein structure predictors, particularly for the challenging CDR-H3 loop which is critical for antigen binding. ImmuneBuilder has become the standard fast method for high-throughput immune protein structural analysis.
datasets: SAbDab (Structural Antibody Database), Protein Data Bank antibody structures

title: Deep-HPI-pred: An R-Shiny applet for network-based classification and prediction of Host-Pathogen protein-protein interactions
summary: Deep-HPI-pred is a web-based R/Shiny application that uses network-driven feature learning to predict unmapped interactions between pathogen and host proteins. The method leverages network topology features—centrality measures, clustering coefficients, and network motifs—derived from known PPI networks to characterize proteins and train deep classifiers. Using citrus and Candidatus Liberibacter asiaticus (CLas) bacteria training datasets as a case study, the applet identifies novel host-pathogen interactions and visualizes interaction networks. The results demonstrate versatility across diverse host-pathogen systems, and the web interface provides accessibility to researchers without computational expertise. Deep-HPI-pred provides a unified service for both interaction prediction and network visualization.
datasets: Citrus-CLas host-pathogen PPI network, HPIDB

title: Improved deep learning prediction of antigen-antibody interactions
venue: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
summary: This PNAS study explores deep learning for computationally predicting antibody-antigen interactions using SARS-CoV-2 spike protein as a primary test case. The approach demonstrates the ability to predict interactions between antibodies and various epitopes on the antigen surface, leveraging antibody sequences collected from B cell sequencing experiments. The study shows promising results for predicting which antibodies bind to which epitopes without requiring structural information, providing insights for antibody development workflows. A key finding is that combining B cell sequencing repertoire data with deep learning enables epitope-specific antibody binding prediction at scale, with significant implications for vaccine development and therapeutic antibody discovery.
datasets: SARS-CoV-2 spike protein antibody interaction data, B cell sequencing data

title: ABodyBuilder3: Improved and scalable antibody structure predictions
summary: ABodyBuilder3 is an improved and scalable version of the ImmuneBuilder ABodyBuilder2 model for antibody structure prediction, achieving new state-of-the-art accuracy in modeling CDR loops by leveraging protein language model (ESM) embeddings. The model introduces careful relaxation strategies that further improve predicted structure quality. A predicted Local Distance Difference Test (pLDDT) confidence score enables identification of low-confidence predictions. ABodyBuilder3 scales to predict structures for massive antibody repertoires, enabling population-level structural analysis of immune responses. The improved CDR-H3 loop accuracy is particularly important for structure-guided antibody engineering and virtual screening of antibody libraries.
datasets: SAbDab benchmark, OAS (Observed Antibody Space) sequences

title: Towards the accurate modelling of antibody-antigen complexes from sequence using machine learning and information-driven docking
summary: This paper analyzes the ability of protein-protein docking tools to use machine learning-generated antibody structures for information-driven docking of antibody-antigen complexes. HADDOCK combined with an ensemble of ML-predicted antibody structures (IgFold, ImmuneBuilder, AlphaFold2) and AlphaFold2-predicted antigen structures achieves accurate antibody-antigen complex models. Targeted docking using CDR region knowledge and epitope information enables high-quality models with reduced sampling, providing a computationally efficient protocol that outperforms the ZDOCK baseline. The study demonstrates that combining ML structure prediction with information-driven docking provides a practical workflow for therapeutic antibody complex modeling when experimental structures are unavailable.
datasets: SAbDab antibody-antigen complex benchmark, ABAG-docking benchmark

title: A comparison of antibody-antigen complex sequence-to-structure prediction methods and their systematic biases
summary: This comparative benchmarking study evaluates six representative methods for predicting antibody-antigen complex structures from sequence, including AlphaFold-Multimer, RoseTTAFold, ClusPro antibody mode, SnugDock, and AbAdapt. AlphaFold-Multimer is found to outperform other methods, though absolute performance leaves considerable room for improvement. A novel structural analysis reveals that AlphaFold-Multimer models of lower quality display biases toward fewer structural matches (TERMs) in non-antibody PDB structures at the interface. The study identifies that scarcity of interfacial geometry data in PDB currently limits machine learning performance on antibody-antigen prediction, pointing to data augmentation strategies as a key research direction.
datasets: SAbDab antibody-antigen complex benchmark

title: DeepInterAware: Deep Interaction Interface-Aware Network for Improving Antigen-Antibody Interaction Prediction from Sequence Data
summary: DeepInterAware proposes a deep learning framework that dynamically incorporates interaction interface information learned from sequence data alongside inherent sequence specificity for antigen-antibody interaction prediction. The framework learns to identify potential binding sites and interfaces from sequence data alone, bypassing the structural data bottleneck. Experimental results show DeepInterAware outperforms existing methods and demonstrates strong inductive capabilities for predicting interactions with unseen antigens or antibodies. It can also detect mutations within antigens or antibodies and extend to predicting binding free energy changes upon mutation. A HER2-targeting antibody screening experiment validates DeepInterAware's capability for identifying binding antibodies for therapeutic target antigens.
datasets: Antibody-antigen interaction datasets, HER2 antibody screening data

title: An Integration of Deep Learning with Feature Embedding for Protein-Protein Interaction Prediction
summary: Proposes DeepFE-PPI, which integrates Res2vec residue-level feature embedding with deep learning for PPI prediction. The Res2vec approach obtains residue-residue interaction features from raw sequence, feeding more effective inputs into a deep learning classifier. Evaluated on S. cerevisiae and human datasets.
datasets: S. cerevisiae (Yeast core), Human PPI datasets

title: Comparing Two Deep Learning Sequence-Based Models for Protein-Protein Interaction Prediction
summary: Systematically compares two carefully designed CNN-based deep learning models for PPI prediction. Identifies pitfalls to avoid in sequence-based PPI prediction, including data leakage and improper dataset splitting. Advocates for rigorous benchmarking practices for deep learning PPI methods.
datasets: Yeast, Human PPI benchmark datasets

title: Multimodal Pre-Training Model for Sequence-based Prediction of Protein-Protein Interaction
summary: Proposes a multimodal pre-training model for PPI prediction combining protein sequence information with structural contact map information. Addresses the limitation that neither sequences nor contact maps fully characterize protein structures and functions relevant for PPI. Uses structure-aware pre-training techniques to learn joint sequence-structure representations.
datasets: Human PPI dataset, SHS27k, SHS148k

title: TAGPPI: Sequence-Based Protein-Protein Interaction Prediction Incorporating Predicted Structure Information
summary: Proposes TAGPPI, which incorporates both sequence features and predicted structural information via graph representation learning on contact maps. Uses tree-augmented graph-based structural features alongside sequence-based features for PPI prediction. Demonstrates improved generalization especially for novel proteins.
datasets: SHS27K, SHS148K, STRING datasets

title: SDNN-PPI: Self-Attention with Deep Neural Network Effect on Protein-Protein Interaction Prediction
summary: Proposes SDNN-PPI combining deep neural networks with self-attention mechanism for PPI prediction. Achieves good performance on interspecific and intraspecific datasets, with cross-species prediction capability. Explores mechanisms of PPI from sequence information.
datasets: Human, Yeast, cross-species PPI datasets

title: MM-StackEns: A New Deep Multimodal Stacked Generalization Approach for Protein-Protein Interaction Prediction
summary: Proposes multimodal stacked generalization ensemble combining siamese neural network for sequence information with graph attention networks for PPI network view. Uses pretrained language model embeddings for improved generalization. Demonstrates superiority on Yeast datasets and competitive performance on Human datasets.
datasets: Yeast, Human PPI datasets (multiple splits)

title: xCAPT5: protein–protein interaction prediction using deep and wide multi-kernel pooling convolutional neural networks with protein language model
summary: xCAPT5 leverages T5-XL-UniRef50 protein large language model for generating rich amino acid embeddings. Uses a multi-kernel deep convolutional siamese neural network integrating XGBoost. Achieves superior performance in binary PPI prediction with robust generalization across intra-species, cross-species, inter-species, and stringent similarity contexts. Represents early integration of large protein language model embeddings into PPI classification.
datasets: Human, mouse, and cross-species PPI benchmark datasets

title: TUnA: An Uncertainty-Aware Transformer Model for Sequence-Based Protein-Protein Interaction Prediction
summary: Presents TUnA, a Transformer-based model with uncertainty awareness for PPI prediction. Uses ESM-2 embeddings with Transformer architecture, provides uncertainty estimates alongside predictions. Addresses generalization to proteins absent from training set, enabling more reliable predictions.
datasets: SHS27K, SHS148K, Human, Yeast PPI datasets

title: Prediction of Protein-Protein Interactions Based on Integrating Deep Learning and Feature Fusion
summary: Integrates deep learning with feature fusion combining handcrafted features (AAC, Local Descriptors, Pseudo-AAC) with sequence embeddings learned by Doc2vec. Achieves 96.34% and 99.30% accuracy on Yeast and Human datasets respectively. Demonstrates improved generalization on cross-species evaluation.
datasets: Yeast, Human, C. elegans, H. pylori, M. musculus, E. coli datasets

title: MPPRL: Multi-Perspective Protein Representation Learning for Protein-Protein Interaction Prediction
summary: Presents MPPRL framework using ESM-2 for protein sequence feature extraction with self-attention encoders for long-range contexts, cross-protein transformer modules for fine-grained interactions, and graph-convolutional encoding for spatial structure. Multi-perspective fusion improves PPI prediction.
datasets: PPI benchmark datasets including Human, Yeast

title: Evaluating Protein Transfer Learning with TAPE
summary: Introduces TAPE (Tasks Assessing Protein Embeddings), a benchmark suite of five biologically relevant semi-supervised learning tasks for protein representation learning. Benchmarks transformer, LSTM, and CNN models with self-supervised pretraining. Shows self-supervised pretraining more than doubles performance on some tasks. Foundational benchmark for protein LMs including PPI-related tasks.
datasets: UniRef90 pretraining; secondary structure, remote homology, fluorescence, stability, contact prediction tasks

title: Unified Rational Protein Engineering with Sequence-Based Deep Representation Learning (UniRep)
summary: Presents UniRep, a multiplicative LSTM trained on ~24 million UniRef50 sequences. Learns a universal protein representation capturing structural, evolutionary, and biophysical properties. Broadly applicable to protein engineering tasks including stability prediction and function prediction with 100x efficiency improvement over directed evolution.
datasets: UniRef50 (~24M sequences), protein stability (Rocklin), GB1 fitness

title: Learning Protein Sequence Embeddings Using Information from Structure (Bepler & Berger)
summary: Introduces bidirectional LSTM protein sequence embeddings trained with two-part feedback from global structural similarity and residue contact maps. Defines soft symmetric alignment (SSA) for learning from structural similarity without position-level correspondence. Achieves state-of-the-art structural similarity prediction and improves transmembrane domain prediction.
datasets: ASTRAL SCOP, ProteinNet, UniRef90; SCOPe structural similarity benchmarks

title: A High Efficient Biological Language Model for Predicting Protein-Protein Interactions
summary: Proposes a biological language processing model (Bio2Vec + CNN) for PPI prediction using only protein sequences. Bio2Vec obtains protein sequence features using 'bio-word' context in a fashion analogous to word2vec. Demonstrates efficient and accurate PPI prediction as an early work applying NLP-style language modeling to proteins.
datasets: Pan's human PPI dataset, S. cerevisiae PPI dataset

title: Biological Structure and Function Emerge from Scaling Unsupervised Learning to 250 Million Protein Sequences (ESM-1b)
venue: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences (PNAS)
summary: Presents ESM-1b, a transformer-based protein language model trained on 250 million protein sequences (86 billion amino acids). Discovers biological structure, function, and evolutionary organization from sequence alone. Achieves state-of-the-art in contact prediction, mutation effect, and secondary structure; representations improve PPI prediction downstream.
datasets: UniRef50 (250M sequences), TAPE benchmarks, ProteinNet

title: ProtTrans: Toward Understanding the Language of Life Through Self-Supervised Learning
venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
summary: Presents ProtTrans: six protein language models (ProtBERT, ProtAlbert, ProtXLNet, ProtT5, ProtElectra, ProtBERT-BFD) trained on 393 billion amino acids using the Summit supercomputer. Demonstrates state-of-the-art performance on secondary structure prediction, subcellular location, and membrane binding; widely used for PPI prediction via embeddings.
datasets: UniRef100/UniRef50/BFD (393B amino acids), TAPE benchmarks

title: Learning the Protein Language: Evolution, Structure, and Function (ProSE)
summary: Presents ProSE (Protein Sequence Embeddings) using multi-task and masked language model training on sequences. Models learn evolutionary, structural, and functional organization of protein space. ProSE embeddings enable state-of-the-art structure prediction and improve downstream function prediction via transfer learning, including PPI tasks.
datasets: UniRef90, CATH structural datasets, SCOP homology benchmarks

title: ProteinBERT: A Universal Deep-Learning Model of Protein Sequence and Function
summary: Presents ProteinBERT, a protein-specific BERT with novel architecture combining local and global representations. Pretraining uses masked language modeling plus Gene Ontology (GO) annotation prediction. Highly efficient for very long sequences. Fine-tuned on multiple tasks including secondary structure and PPI-related annotation tasks.
datasets: UniRef90 (~106M sequences), GO annotations; 9 downstream benchmarks

title: Learning Inverse Folding from Millions of Predicted Structures (ESM-IF1)
summary: Presents ESM-IF1, a sequence-to-sequence transformer with invariant geometric input processing for inverse folding. Trained on 12M AlphaFold2-predicted structures. Achieves 51% native sequence recovery (72% for buried residues). Effective for zero-shot prediction of PPI complex stability and binding affinity mutations.
datasets: 12M AlphaFold2-predicted structures + CATH experimental structures

title: Evolutionary-Scale Prediction of Atomic-Level Protein Structure with a Language Model (ESM-2/ESMFold)
summary: Presents ESM-2 language models (up to 15B parameters) and ESMFold structure predictor. Shows that scaled language models learn information enabling atomic-level 3D protein structure prediction. Up to 60x faster than AlphaFold2 while maintaining accuracy. ESM-2 embeddings widely adopted for downstream PPI prediction tasks.
datasets: UniRef50/UniRef90 (670M sequences), CAMEO, CASP14

title: SaProt: Protein Language Modeling with Structure-aware Vocabulary
summary: SaProt is a protein language model that incorporates structural information using a structure-aware vocabulary combining amino acid tokens with structural tokens from Foldseek. Pre-trained on ~40M protein sequences with Foldseek-derived structural information. Achieves state-of-the-art performance on multiple downstream tasks including mutation effect prediction, protein function annotation, and protein-protein interaction prediction.
datasets: UniRef50, AlphaFoldDB structures, multiple benchmark datasets

title: Fine-Tuning Protein Language Models Boosts Predictions Across Diverse Tasks
summary: Systematically compares fine-tuning of three protein language models (ESM2, ProtT5, Ankh) on eight tasks. Demonstrates that task-specific supervised fine-tuning almost always improves over using frozen embeddings. Back-propagation to the language model is critical. Establishes fine-tuning as de facto standard for applying PLMs including PPI prediction.
datasets: Eight downstream tasks including secondary structure, binding site, PPI, solubility prediction

title: KSGPPI: Improving Protein-Protein Interaction Prediction Using Protein Language Model and Protein Network Features
summary: KSGPPI improves PPI prediction by combining ESM-2 protein language model with protein interaction network graph embedding features. First module uses ESM-2 for global complex patterns and CKSAAP+2D-CNN for local information. Second module finds similar proteins in STRING via NW-align and captures graph embedding via Node2vec. Achieves average prediction accuracy of 88.96% on benchmark datasets with high MCC values.
datasets: Human PPI benchmarks, STRING database

title: MINT: Learning the Language of Protein-Protein Interactions
summary: Introduces MINT, a PLM specifically designed to model sets of interacting proteins contextually. Trained unsupervised on STRING-derived PPI dataset. Outperforms existing PLMs in binding affinity prediction, mutational effect estimation, antibody-antigen modeling, and T cell receptor-epitope binding prediction. Key advance in interaction-aware protein language modeling.
datasets: STRING PPI database (training), PDBbind, SKEMPI2, AbDb, VDJdb (evaluation)

title: PLM-interact: Extending Protein Language Models to Predict Protein-Protein Interactions
summary: Extends ESM-2 to directly model protein-protein interactions by handling paired protein sequences with longer context and next-sentence prediction fine-tuning. PLM-interact outperforms TUnA, TT3D, Topsy-Turvy, D-SCRIPT, PIPR, and DeepPPI on multi-species datasets. Can predict mutation effects on interactions and enables cross-species transfer learning.
datasets: Multi-species PPI dataset (Human, Mouse, Fly, Worm, Yeast, E. coli); mutation effect benchmarks

title: Graph-Based Prediction of Protein-Protein Interactions with Attributed Signed Graph Embedding (S-VGAE)
summary: Introduces signed variational graph auto-encoder (S-VGAE) for PPI prediction combining sequence information and graph structure. Learns graph topology features with signed graph embedding. Achieves 99.15% accuracy on HPRD dataset and state-of-the-art results on Human, Yeast, C. elegans, and Drosophila datasets.
datasets: HPRD, Human, Yeast, C. elegans, Drosophila PPI datasets

title: Graph Neural Network for Inter-Novel-Protein Interaction Prediction (GNN-PPI)
summary: Proposes GNN-PPI, which models protein correlations using GNN on a PPI network graph for multi-label protein interaction prediction. Introduces a new evaluation protocol that specifically assesses inter-novel-protein interactions (BFS/DFS splits). Demonstrates that GNN significantly outperforms sequence-only methods especially for novel proteins.
datasets: SHS27K, SHS148K datasets from STRING

title: Predicting Protein-Protein Interactions via Gated Graph Attention Signed Network
summary: Models PPI as a signed network (positive/negative effects) and applies gated graph attention for link prediction. Represents proteins as nodes in a signed graph with edges indicating positive or negative interactions. Demonstrates accurate PPI prediction for pharmacogenomics and signal transduction applications.
datasets: Human PPI signed network datasets

title: An Efficient Computational Model for Large-Scale PPI Prediction Based on Accurate and Scalable Graph Embedding (LPPI/GraphSAGE)
summary: Develops LPPI using graph embedding methods (including GraphSAGE) to learn topology features from reconstructed PPI graphs for large-scale prediction. Achieves 0.99997 accuracy on PPI network dataset. Demonstrates scalability and efficiency for large-scale PPI prediction in biological applications.
datasets: PPI network datasets, GraphSAGE benchmark dataset

title: Prediction of Protein-Protein Interaction Using Graph Neural Networks
summary: Applies GCN and GAT models to PPI prediction using protein structural information from PDB files and protein language model node features. Builds protein graphs from 3D coordinates where residues are nodes. Validates on Human and other PPI datasets, demonstrating structure-based graph approaches outperform sequence-only methods.
datasets: Human PPI dataset, other benchmark datasets

title: Graph Neural Network for Protein-Protein Interaction Prediction: A Comparative Study
summary: Presents systematic comparative study of five GNN architectures for PPI prediction: NN, GCN, GAT, hyperbolic neural networks (HNN), and hyperbolic graph convolutions (HGCN). Analyzes strengths and weaknesses of each approach. Provides insight into which graph neural network architectures best capture PPI network topology.
datasets: Human PPI network, Yeast PPI network

title: Hierarchical graph learning for protein-protein interaction
summary: HIGH-PPI presents a double-viewed hierarchical graph learning model for PPI prediction incorporating both outside-protein (PPI network) and inside-protein (residue chemical descriptor) views. Creates hierarchical graphs where PPI network nodes are protein graphs. Demonstrates high accuracy in PPI prediction and interpretation of modes of action including binding/catalytic site identification.
datasets: Human interactome, SHS27k, SHS148k, custom interaction benchmarks

title: SemiGNN-PPI: Self-Ensembling Multi-Graph Neural Network for Efficient and Generalizable Protein-Protein Interaction Prediction
summary: Proposes SemiGNN-PPI combining GNN with Mean Teacher for semi-supervised learning, modeling protein correlations and label dependencies through multiple graphs from feature and label perspectives. Designs multiple graph consistency constraints for student-teacher alignment. Outperforms state-of-the-art methods especially with limited annotations and unseen proteins.
datasets: SHS27K, SHS148K datasets

title: HNSPPI: A Hybrid Computational Model Combining Network and Sequence Information for PPI Prediction
summary: Proposes HNSPPI, a hybrid supervised model integrating amino acid sequence information with connection properties from PPI networks. Comprehensively characterizes intrinsic protein relationships by combining sequence features with network topology. Applied to SARS-CoV-2-Human interactions, outperforms five existing algorithms on six benchmark datasets.
datasets: Six benchmark PPI datasets; SARS-CoV-2-Human interaction dataset

title: Knowledge Graph Embedding for Profiling the Interaction Between Transcription Factors and Target Genes
summary: Formulates transcription factor-target gene interaction prediction as multi-label link type classification on a heterogeneous knowledge graph. Uses KGE-TGI model trained in multi-task learning on a specially constructed knowledge graph incorporating gene expression data. Demonstrates knowledge graph embedding approach for specialized protein interaction networks.
datasets: Custom TF-TGI knowledge graph; gene expression datasets

title: DeepRank-GNN: A Graph Neural Network Framework to Learn Patterns in Protein-Protein Interfaces
summary: Presents DeepRank-GNN converting protein-protein interfaces from PDB 3D coordinates into atomic or residue-scale rotation-invariant graphs processed by GNNs. Overcomes CNN limitations of non-rotation invariance. Learns patterns in PPI interfaces for binding quality assessment, applicable to docking evaluation.
datasets: PDB-based protein complex datasets; CAPRI docking benchmark

title: GNNGL-PPI: Multi-Category Prediction of Protein-Protein Interactions Using Graph Neural Networks Based on Global Graphs and Local Subgraphs
summary: Proposes GNNGL-PPI for multi-category PPI prediction using Graph Isomorphism Network (GIN) for global graph features and GIN-AK for local subgraph features. Introduces Asymmetric Loss function for class imbalance. Outperforms state-of-the-art methods on SHS27K and SHS148K datasets under three partitioning algorithms.
datasets: SHS27K, SHS148K from STRING

title: MGPPI: Multiscale Graph Neural Networks for Explainable Protein-Protein Interaction Prediction
summary: Proposes MGPPI using multiscale GNN to capture local and global protein structural information. Introduces Grad-WAM (Gradient Weighted Interaction Activation Mapping) for interpretability, highlighting key binding residue sites. Applied to SARS-CoV-2 spike-ACE2 interaction and cancer biomarker identification.
datasets: Multiple structural PPI datasets; SARS-CoV-2 spike-ACE2 dataset; cancer patient survival data

title: Hierarchical Graph Learning for Protein-Protein Interaction via Label-Aware Hierarchical Subgraph (laruGL-PPI)
summary: Proposes laruGL-PPI addressing topological shortcut problems in GNN-based PPI prediction. Uses edge-based subgraph sampling and hierarchical graph modeling with label graph for interaction type dependencies. Scalable to large PPI datasets. Demonstrates superior performance especially for unseen proteins.
datasets: SHS27K, SHS148K datasets

title: DSSGNN-PPI: A Protein-Protein Interactions Prediction Model Based on Double Structure and Sequence Graph Neural Networks
summary: Proposes DSSGNN-PPI using distance graph between amino acid residues fed into underlying GAT module for structure embedding, combined with sequence representation. Fuses structural and sequence features into high-level protein complex networks for multi-type PPI classification. Achieves superior multi-type interaction prediction.
datasets: SHS27K, SHS148K, STRING-based multi-type PPI datasets

title: Graph Neural Networks for Protein-Protein Interactions: A Short Survey
summary: Reviews GNN methods for PPI prediction, classifying approaches into GNN/GCN-based and GAT/Graph Auto-Encoder-based categories. Discusses applications, advantages, and limitations of different graph neural network architectures for PPI prediction. Provides landscape of the field as of 2024.
datasets: Survey of multiple datasets used across reviewed methods

title: SpatialPPIv2: Enhancing Protein-Protein Interaction Prediction Through Graph Neural Networks with Protein Language Models
summary: Presents SpatialPPIv2, a GAT-based model using large protein language models for sequence feature embedding and graph attention to capture structural information. Uses PINDER dataset combining RCSB PDB and AlphaFold. No longer depends on structure prediction algorithms, robust across AlphaFold3/2 and ESMFold structures.
datasets: PINDER dataset (RCSB PDB + AlphaFold structures)

title: HyboWaveNet: Hyperbolic Graph Neural Networks with Multi-Scale Wavelet Transform for PPI Prediction
summary: Proposes HyboWaveNet combining hyperbolic graph neural networks and multiscale wavelet transforms for PPI prediction. Maps protein features to Lorentz space to capture hierarchical biological relationships. Integrates wavelet transforms for adaptive extraction of local and global interaction features across different resolutions.
datasets: Standard PPI benchmark datasets
